title: VS Code surface (v0.17): Memory panel + Journey + Why/Capture — the full file claim
context: The v0.17 surface record needed to claim every file the surface owns: the Journey view (journey.ts) and its data source (src/wiki/graph.ts — the wiki's embedded memory-graph JSON is the one data source, carried forward from the superseded Journey-era chain) alongside the panel/README files, so no file remains anchored only through superseded links.
decision: The extension's public contract is three surfaces: (1) the Hunch Memory activity-bar panel — memory-move timeline with click-to-diff and local revert, ⚖ Needs-your-decision escalations, 🏛 Constitution group with one-click audited activate/demote/withdraw/retire, strictness switch, approve-to-push; (2) Journey — the read-only compounding view embedding the interactive memory graph over a postMessage bridge, fed solely by the wiki's embedded JSON; (3) Why/Capture — the per-file read and the delegated write. All writes delegate to the hunch CLI/MCP; the panel spawn seam is conformance-certified against CLI/MCP/CI receipts.
consequences: journey.ts and src/wiki/graph.ts anchor to the live decision — the remaining anchor-stale drift closes.
Future surface changes supersede THIS record on the same topic.
alternatives_rejected: A second topic for the Journey files (rejected: one surface, one chain — splitting topics would hide that the panel and Journey ship as one contract and would re-create cross-chain staleness)
Leaving Journey files on the superseded chain links (rejected: grounding then cites dead intent for live files)
Renaming the topic to merge with vscode-extension.scope (rejected: renames break anchor history; superseded links must stay auditable)
